page: Amazon Web Services m5a.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering two vCPUs from a single physical core with two threads per core. The CPU operates at a speed of 2.5 GHz and includes 8 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports hardware features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and SHA-NI instructions, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network performance is characterized by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and it does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5a.large demonstrates entry-level performance. Synthetic CPU tests, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core suites, place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor throughput, with read-only large block operations reaching 22.95 GB/sec and write operations falling into the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are positioned in the lower tier of benchmarks.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the m5a.large offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require high-performance compute or memory bandwidth. It is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale development or testing environments, and low-throughput background tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with two vCPUs and one physical core running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is limited to a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower tier, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. Consequently, the m5a.large represents a budget-friendly, entry-level option for workloads with modest resource demands. It is best suited for light general-purpose applications, development environments, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, offering a budget-friendly option for light workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, suited for light workloads.
tagline: An entry level general purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory configuration for light utility workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for light web serving and development environments
